points.text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
The disease spread worldwide starting in January 2020, leading to a pandemic.
The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020.
COVID-19 is caused by infection with SARS-CoV-2.
Transmission occurs mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets or aerosols.
COVID-19 is an illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, which emerged in late 2019 and became a pandemic in 2020.
The virus spreads mainly through close contact and tiny droplets from coughs, sneezes, or talking.
Many people experience mild illness or no symptoms, but it can be severe for older adults and those with certain conditions.
Complications from severe COVID-19 can include respiratory distress, heart issues, and blood clots.
COVID-19 can lead to long-term symptoms, known as post-COVID-19 syndrome, which is still under research.
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by a coronavirus called SARS-CoV-2.
'CO' stands for corona, 'VI' for virus, and 'D' for disease.
Symptoms may appear 2 to 14 days after exposure to the virus and can range from very mild to severe illness.
Some people who have been infected don’t have any symptoms.
COVID-19 has been described as a 'pandemic', referring to its geographical spread.
